body: Convolution reverb reproduces a real space by processing the signal with an impulse response - a recording of how that space responds to a click. Load hall, room or plate IRs (or FX impulses) and the dry signal takes on that space, in real time at low latency.

A spectral linear-phase EQ shapes the reverb (or the dry signal) without introducing phase shift, which keeps transients tight on a mix bus. Time-stretch changes the impulse's length and pitch, and envelopes shape the tail's volume, pan and stereo width.
